instruction:
In simple linear regression, most often we perform a two-tail test of the population slope $\beta_1$ to determine whether there is sufficient evidence to infer that a linear relationship exists. The null hypothesis is stated as: [ANS] A. $H_0: \beta_1=0$  B. $H_0: \beta_1=\rho_s$  C. $H_0: \beta_1=b_1$  D. $H_0: \beta_1=r$
Given the least squares regression line $\hat{y}$=5-2x: [ANS] A. as x decreases, so does y  B. the relationship between x and y is positive  C. the relationship between x and y is negative  D. as x increases, so does y
